body.title: Pursuit — canonical term for goal-bound behavioral units (naming decision)
body.result: Name 'Pursuit' decided by Akien 2026-04-22 ('i like persuit. that's perfect'). Canonical term for goal-bound behavioral units above engrams. Closes on filing per decision design; unblocks design-doc and test-case.
body.description:
Formally records 'Pursuit' as the canonical term for the goal-bound behavioral layer above engrams in Igor's cognitive architecture.

Definition settled in 2026-04-22 design conversation:
- A Pursuit is a goal-bound unit of execution that combines: (1) commitment event with anticipatory dopamine trace, (2) engram-substrate action(s), (3) recursive sub-Pursuit spawning, (4) completion test, (5) dopamine ding on success.
- Goals are not just big ambitions — every act of commitment, however small, IS a Pursuit. 'Reply to Akien.' 'Scan for work.' 'Claim T-x.'
- Pursuits ORCHESTRATE engrams; they are not an engram template. Different kind of object: engrams execute, Pursuits pursue.

Layer stack:
- Cells (engram primitives / 'assembly')
- Engrams (behavior chains / 'high-level code')
- Pursuits (goal-bound orchestration of engrams)

Biology root:
- Dopamine fires at commitment (anticipation burst), sub-goal completion, and final completion — not just at the end.
- Cortico-basal-ganglia loops recruit cortical microcircuits (same relationship Pursuits have to engrams).
- Closest formal terms: TOTE units (Miller-Galanter-Pribram), Options (Sutton-Precup RL). 'Pursuit' is our term.

Scope: this ticket is the naming record. Closes on filing — name is settled. It gates T-single-pursuit-test-case, T-pursuit-layer-design-doc, and T-pursuit-engram-programming-docs.

Test plan: verify 'Pursuit' propagates correctly into palace, design docs, and engram programming docs. No code changes in this ticket.
